title: eragrostis pectinacea subsp. felzinesii
intro: Eragrostis pectinacea subsp. felzinesii, also known as a subspecies of tufted lovegrass, is a perennial grass native to the central and eastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Eragrostis pectinacea subsp. felzinesii include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infection. Eragrostis pectinacea subsp. felzinesii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
